instructions: Below are a number of characteristics that may or may not apply to you. Please indicate the extent to which you agree or disagree with each statement.

Openness.label: Openness to Experience
Openness.overview: Openness reflects intellectual curiosity, imagination, and appreciation for art, emotion, and novel experiences.
Openness.highMeaning: High scorers tend to be creative, curious about the world, and open to unconventional ideas.
Openness.lowMeaning: Low scorers tend to prefer routine, practical matters, and conventional approaches.

Conscientiousness.label: Conscientiousness
Conscientiousness.overview: Conscientiousness measures self-discipline, organization, and goal-directed behavior.
Conscientiousness.highMeaning: High scorers are typically organized, reliable, and persistent in pursuing goals.
Conscientiousness.lowMeaning: Low scorers may be more spontaneous, flexible, and less focused on structure.

Extraversion.label: Extraversion
Extraversion.overview: Extraversion captures energy drawn from social interaction and external stimulation.
Extraversion.highMeaning: High scorers are typically outgoing, energetic, and enjoy being around people.
Extraversion.lowMeaning: Low scorers (introverts) tend to be reserved, prefer solitude, and recharge through quiet time.

Agreeableness.label: Agreeableness
Agreeableness.overview: Agreeableness reflects concern for social harmony, empathy, and cooperation.
Agreeableness.highMeaning: High scorers are typically compassionate, trusting, and value getting along with others.
Agreeableness.lowMeaning: Low scorers may be more competitive, skeptical, and prioritize truth over harmony.

Neuroticism.label: Neuroticism
Neuroticism.overview: Neuroticism measures emotional reactivity and sensitivity to stress.
Neuroticism.highMeaning: High scorers are more likely to experience negative emotions like anxiety, anger, or sadness.
Neuroticism.lowMeaning: Low scorers (emotionally stable) tend to be calm, even-tempered, and resilient under stress.

patterns.name: Creative Stability
patterns.description: This combination suggests intellectual curiosity and creative engagement without anxiety interference. You likely explore new ideas with confidence and emotional resilience.

patterns.name: Conscientious Introvert
patterns.description: This profile suggests someone who is organized and goal-focused, but energized by solitary work rather than group settings. You may excel in roles requiring sustained concentration.

patterns.name: Agreeable Extrovert
patterns.description: This combination reflects strong people skills — you're both socially energized and empathetically attuned. You likely thrive in collaborative, relationship-focused roles.

patterns.name: Organized Creative
patterns.description: This rare combination suggests disciplined creativity — you have both imaginative vision and the follow-through to realize it. You likely balance big-picture thinking with practical execution.

Openness.High: Seek environments that reward creativity and intellectual exploration. Consider roles in research, arts, or innovation.
Openness.Moderate: You balance novelty with practicality. Look for settings that offer variety within structure.
Openness.Low: You may excel in roles requiring consistency and proven methods. Seek clear, established processes.

Conscientiousness.High: Your discipline is a strength — but watch for perfectionism or rigidity. Build in time for rest and spontaneity.
Conscientiousness.Moderate: You balance structure with flexibility. This adaptability serves you well in dynamic environments.
Conscientiousness.Low: Consider systems or accountability partners to support follow-through on long-term goals.

Extraversion.High: You thrive on social energy. Seek roles with collaboration and interaction, but schedule downtime to recharge.
Extraversion.Moderate: You're socially flexible. Leverage this balance in roles requiring both teamwork and independent work.
Extraversion.Low: Your energy comes from solitude. Seek roles allowing deep focus, and honor your need for quiet.

Agreeableness.High: Your empathy is a strength — but practice setting boundaries to avoid overextending yourself.
Agreeableness.Moderate: You balance cooperation with assertiveness. This serves you well in roles requiring diplomacy.
Agreeableness.Low: Your directness and skepticism can be assets — pair them with empathy to build trust in relationships.

Neuroticism.High: Your emotional sensitivity can be channeled into empathy and depth. Consider stress-management practices.
Neuroticism.Moderate: You experience a typical range of emotional ups and downs. Build resilience through self-awareness.
Neuroticism.Low: Your emotional stability is a strength. Share your calm perspective to support others in stressful times.

questions.text: I am the life of the party.
I am not interested in other people's problems.
I get chores done right away.
I often forget to put things back in their proper place.
I am relaxed most of the time.
I am not interested in abstract ideas.
I talk to a lot of different people at parties.
I don't like to draw attention to myself.
I don't mind being the center of attention.
I am not really interested in others.
I take time out for others.
I make people feel at ease.
I feel little concern for others.
I make a mess of things.
I am exacting in my work.
I change my mood a lot.
I have difficulty understanding abstract ideas.
I do not have a good imagination.
I am quick to understand things.
I spend time reflecting on things.
I am not interested in theoretical discussions.
